topic:
Evaluation Guide § Falsifiability — post-fix review (derives_from_dome count, Schumann range,

tag)
change_summary:
Decider fixed the 'eg-falsifiability' (#eg-falsifiability) section in build-scripts/generate-html.js: (a) derives_from_dome count corrected from 4 to 2 WINs; WIN-020 and WIN-058 removed from the named list, leaving WIN-044 and WIN-057; (b) Schumann prediction corrected from '22 Hz' to a '22–35 Hz' range; (c) a missing

tag was fixed so the section's paragraph nesting is now balanced.
our_argument_summary: The Evaluation Guide's falsifiability section states four conditions any of which would upgrade a verdict. Condition 1 asks for a discriminating prediction derived from dome geometry; the section now states that only 2 of ${counts.total} (=69) claims even attempt this, naming WIN-044 and WIN-057. Condition 3 asks for a derivation of 7.83 Hz from dome geometry, stating the dome's own exponentially-decaying cavity predicts a fundamental in the '22–35 Hz' band when the stated dimensions are used honestly. An Eclipse pre-commitment paragraph at the end pins down the August 2026 annular eclipse as the concrete near-term falsification/corroboration test.

derives_from_dome_count.method: Counted wins.json entries with code_analysis.derives_from_dome === true
derives_from_dome_count.result: Exactly 2 (WIN-044 'Firmament Scaling Function from V12 geometry', WIN-057 'Two-zone disc topology'). WIN-020 and WIN-058 are both tagged derives_from_dome=false in the current data. The body's '2 of ${counts.total}' and the TLDR's 'only 2 even attempt condition 1' are factually consistent with the data as tagged.

schumann_range.method: Cross-checked against WIN-002 detail_evidence, which gives the derivation: H_eff (linear radial avg) ≈ 3,316 km → f ≈ 22.6 Hz; H_eff (area-weighted) ≈ 2,140 km → f ≈ 35 Hz.
schumann_range.result: The '22–35 Hz' range is the span between reasonable spatial averages of H(r) = 8,537·exp(−r/8,619) applied to the quarter-wave formula f = c/(4H). Correct, and more honest than the prior single '22 Hz' phrasing which corresponded only to the linear-radial average.

p_tag_balance.method:
Counted

tags in the rendered #eg-falsifiability

block in docs/index.html.

holes_found.description: WIN-044 is mischaracterized in the justifying clause. The body says '... (WIN-044, WIN-057), and both produce values that either match standard physics or contradict measurement.' WIN-057 fits that description — its verdict is 'Misleading' and it is ~600× worse than standard geodesy (6.2% RMSE vs. Vincenty's sub-0.01%), so 'contradicts measurement' is defensible. But WIN-044's verdict is 'Self-Contradicted' because it is an internal model derivation of eclipse anomaly values that have not yet been measured (the prediction targets the August 2026 eclipse). Its finding text is explicit: 'Internal model derivation claimed as confirmed WIN before observational test (eclipse August 2026); tests self-consistency, not truth.' So WIN-044 neither 'matches standard physics' nor 'contradicts measurement' at this moment — the measurement does not yet exist. The accurate criticism of WIN-044 is circularity / internal-consistency-masquerading-as-prediction, not measurement mismatch. A dome defender could seize on this: 'You concede the measurement is pending (eclipse 2026) yet already claim the values contradict it — which is it?' This undermines the very falsifiability claim the section is trying to make.
holes_found.recommendation: Change the tail clause from 'and both produce values that either match standard physics or contradict measurement' to something that handles the two WINs separately. Suggested: '... (WIN-044, WIN-057). WIN-057's derived value is ~600× worse than standard geodesy; WIN-044 is an internal-consistency derivation whose target observation (the August 2026 eclipse anomaly) has not yet been measured, so it cannot count as a confirmed prospective prediction until that measurement is made — which is precisely the Condition 2 test described below.' This also doubles as a helpful internal hyperlink between Conditions 1 and 2.

holes_found.description: 'Attempt' is doing too much work. The section says 'only 2 of 69 claims even attempt a dome-geometry derivation.' A charitable dome defender would read 'attempt' as 'try to derive,' and point to WIN-012 ('Mag-gravity coupling 1.67' — explicitly presents κ derivation), WIN-056 ('Solar elevation from H(r)' — claim literally names H(r) as the input), WIN-058b ('Bedrock Induction Ratio κ = 1.677' — registered as PRED-KAPPA-001), and WIN-067 ('Antarctic gravity hole') as further attempts. Our own data tags these derives_from_dome=false because, on investigation, the attempts fail or secretly use globe inputs (WIN-056 actually uses 23.45° globe axial tilt; WIN-012's κ is back-derived from unconfirmed Mohe data; WIN-058b is post-hoc-fit). That is a valid distinction, but 'attempt' in natural English denotes trying, not succeeding. A defender could reasonably argue the section conflates 'successful dome-geometry derivation' with 'attempted one,' and that the true count of attempts is at least 6–8.
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the language from 'even attempt' to something like 'successfully derive from dome geometry' or 'produce a value traceable to dome geometry without borrowing globe inputs.' Or keep 'attempt' and add a one-line footnote: 'Several additional WINs (e.g., WIN-012, WIN-056, WIN-058b) claim dome-derived values but, on inspection, either back-derive from unconfirmed measurements, use globe formulas in disguise, or post-hoc-fit to observations. We count only WINs whose predicted value traces cleanly from the dome's stated geometric parameters without such smuggling.' This pre-empts the 'you miscounted attempts' attack.

holes_found.description: The literal '2' is hardcoded. The body uses ${counts.total} (good — computed) for 69, but still writes a hardcoded '2' in 'only 2 of ${counts.total} claims' and in the TLDR's 'only 2 even attempt condition 1.' Our project explicitly criticizes the dome for hardcoding counts that can be derived from the data; the CLAUDE.md 'Computed Counts' rule says this extends to ALL project files. If the curmudgeon (or a later analyst pass) re-tags one more WIN to derives_from_dome=true — say, pushing WIN-058b across the line — these two sentences silently become wrong.
holes_found.recommendation: In generate-html.js, add a computed count: const derivesCount = wins.filter(w => w.code_analysis && w.code_analysis.derives_from_dome === true).length; and interpolate it. Then also emit a computed list for the named examples: const derivesIds = wins.filter(...).map(w => 'WIN-' + w.id).join(', ') — so the named IDs in the parenthetical update automatically too.

holes_found.description: Internal inconsistency of Schumann phrasing across the site. The falsifiability section now says '22–35 Hz.' WIN-029's finding still says '~22 Hz, not 7.83,' WIN-038's finding says '~22 Hz baseline,' WIN-061's verdict_text says '~22 Hz baseline, not 7.83 Hz,' and WIN-002's finding says '~22 Hz' in the short finding field even though its detail_verdict_text uses the correct 22–35 range. The 22–35 range is the more defensible, more cautious framing; the individual WIN findings should match. Not a contradiction — a site-wide inconsistency. A defender who notices only the '~22 Hz' figures could claim we quietly widened the range in the falsifiability tab to make Condition 3 look more demanding.
holes_found.recommendation: Non-blocking cleanup. In a future analyst pass, update the short finding/verdict fields in WIN-002, WIN-029, WIN-038, WIN-061 to use the 22–35 Hz range (or add 'at minimum ~22 Hz' / 'range 22–35 Hz depending on spatial average'). Not required for this priority-queue item to close.

holes_found.description: Condition 4 micro-claim 'V12/V13 coordinate system claims impressive accuracy' is an aside that's half-complimentary and half-unsourced in-section. The qualifier 'impressive' is the dome's framing, not ours; here it is presented in our own voice. Elsewhere (e.g., WIN-057 finding) we show the accuracy is 6.2% RMSE on fitted routes and ~600× worse than standard geodesy. Calling it 'impressive' in the Evaluation Guide without the counterweight risks a mental-model mismatch for a reader who has not read the WIN detail.
holes_found.recommendation: Soften to 'claims improved accuracy' or 'claims sub-7% RMSE (still ~600× worse than standard geodesy, see WIN-057),' with the cross-reference. One-word change, low risk of regression.

stronger_arguments.description: The Eclipse pre-commitment paragraph already does the right thing (publicly pre-register a prediction band; specify the discriminating window; date-stamp it before the event), but it buries the discriminating window of ~1 nT in the last sentence. Pull that figure into the paragraph's opening — 'Only values above 20 nT magnitude (a ~1 nT discriminating window) would favor the dome over Chapman physics' — because it is the single most important number in the paragraph and non-technical readers will otherwise miss it.

Condition 2 references our own Timestamp Error analysis. That analysis is the site's 'canary' — the wrong-side-of-the-timestamp critique. A stronger phrasing would make explicit that Condition 2 is independently-motivated scientific criterion (prospective prediction is a textbook standard, not something invented for this review), so that if the dome author fixes OTS usage, he cannot claim we shifted the goalposts. Suggest: 'This is the standard criterion for prospective prediction in experimental physics — not a goalpost we invented for this review. It is the same bar pre-registration serves in clinical trials.'

The section would be further hardened by stating what Condition 1 would look like for a specific WIN domain. Currently the reader has to construct the counterfactual. A one-line example — e.g., 'Concretely: if H(r) predicted a Schumann fundamental of X Hz where X is measured and X ≠ 7.83, that would satisfy Condition 1' — closes the loop and makes it obviously engineerable, not impossibly abstract.

kernel_of_truth.description: The section's core claim — that the review is falsifiable and exposes four concrete, auditable upgrade conditions — is correct and, after these fixes, numerically sound. The 2 / 69 derives-from-dome ratio is consistent with the tag data; the Schumann 22–35 Hz band is algebraically recoverable from H(r); the Eclipse pre-commitment is a well-formed, pre-registered, soon-testable prediction. The decider's fix removed two previously-mis-listed WINs (020, 058) and widened a Schumann claim that was too narrow. These are good-faith corrections in the direction of accuracy.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Not applicable — this is our own section, not a dome claim being defended. The kernel of truth is ours; the question is whether we have stated it tightly enough that a motivated opponent cannot score points against the edges. The holes above are where edges remain.

text_fingerprint.source_location: build-scripts/generate-html.js (falsifiability block inside the Evaluation Guide)
text_fingerprint.tldr_sentence: This review is falsifiable. Four concrete conditions would cause us to upgrade a verdict — any one is sufficient. Of the current claims, only 2 even attempt condition 1, and both produce values that match standard physics or fail against measurement.

deeper_analysis: The adversarial angle of attack on this section is not the numbers — those hold up — but the verbal hedges that sit between the numbers. Specifically: (a) the word 'attempt' in Condition 1's justifying clause, (b) the blanket description 'both produce values that either match standard physics or contradict measurement,' and (c) calling V12/V13 'impressive.' Each of these is a place where a dome defender can run a rhetorical wedge: 'your own wording admits X, your own data shows Y, and the two don't match.' The fixes the decider already applied improved the hard numbers (count, Schumann range, markup). The remaining exposure is in the soft-verbal bridges between them. Tightening those bridges — ideally with computed counts for '2' and a per-WIN split of WIN-044 vs. WIN-057 — finishes the job. Separately, because this section is the central falsifiability claim of the entire review, it should be the most-hardened section on the site; a minor_edit pass in the direction of the recommendations above will get it there. No verdict change is warranted; the section's four conditions are correct; the fixes already applied are net-positive.

citations_verified: disc radius 20,015 km — matches raw-text/ + CLAUDE.md
H(r) = 8,537 × exp(−r/8,619) — matches WIN-002 detail_evidence and dome model page
κ = 1.67 nT/μGal — matches WIN-012 claim field
Schumann fundamental 7.83 Hz — standard measured value
August 12, 2026 annular solar eclipse — correct date
Dome eclipse prediction −17 to −21 nT — consistent with raw-text/ predictions
Chapman mechanism magnitude 5–20 nT — consistent with standard eclipse-magnetic literature
Discriminating window ~1 nT — arithmetic check: overlap 17–20 nT (3 nT); only >20 nT favors dome; 20–21 nT band = 1 nT ✓
citations_unchecked: Chapman mechanism 'documented since 1933' — Sydney Chapman's original ionospheric-current work is 1931–1933 so '1933' is within the range; did not chase a specific 1933 paper DOI for this review
